race.name: Reistadløpet
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers above the Arctic Circle where double-poling goes to die — Norway's northernmost marathon demands classical technique or nothing.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations with warm drinks and food

climate.description: Situated above the Arctic Circle at 69°N, temperatures on race day can range from -20°C to -5°C. Arctic wind chill is a major factor across exposed sections. Snow conditions are typically cold and fast, but polar weather systems can bring sudden whiteouts.
challenges: Extreme Arctic cold, especially in headwind sections
Limited daylight in early season — race held near spring equinox
Wind chill on exposed plateaus and lake crossings

course.primary: Point-to-point through sub-Arctic terrain with sustained climbing that makes double-poling virtually impossible. The course demands genuine classical technique — diagonal stride, herringbone, and kick-and-glide are essential. Rolling hills through birch forest and open tundra.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through Arctic forest and open terrain
course.grooming: Ski Classics standard, well-groomed classic tracks
features: Northernmost Ski Classics race in the world
Sustained climbs that eliminate double-poling as a viable strategy
Arctic birch forest and open tundra landscape
Midnight sun region — race held as light returns in spring
Genuine test of classical skiing technique

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A true classical skier's race — the terrain eliminates double-poling shortcuts. Arctic location delivers reliable snow and dramatic scenery but limits accessibility.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes from T3 to T2

history.summary: Founded in 1964 in the Troms region of northern Norway, Reistadløpet has become one of the most respected classical skiing races in Scandinavia. Its inclusion in the Ski Classics series elevated its international profile. The race is named after the Reistad area near Bardufoss and is celebrated for its uncompromising terrain that rewards traditional diagonal-stride technique.
notable_facts: Northernmost race in the Ski Classics series at 69°N latitude
Course design makes double-poling nearly impossible — a rarity in modern marathon skiing
Located near a Norwegian military base in Bardufoss
Has become a cult favorite among purist classical skiers
Part of the Ski Classics Pro Tour since the series began
